title: be going to (do)
desc: 계획된 의도 또는 근거 있는 예측
examples: I just checked in to my hotel in Seoul and I'm pretty hungry so I'm going to go out and see what restaurants are nearby.
I got a huge bowl of foe and a nice cold beer. I know this is going to hit the spot.
I had an amazing first day in Seoul but man, I'm exhausted so I'm going to cut into this gorgeous cake that one of the students' moms made for me.
It just seems wrong to cut into my own face. I'm going to change into my PJs and go to sleep early tonight.
We're going to be a lucky winner today.

title: will / shall
examples: 20 lucky people will win books or free drinks or free snacks.
I keep telling myself to take deep breaths and it'll all be okay.

title: can / could / be able to
examples: I set up a small market for the kids so they could practice using English naturally while buying toys and snacks.
So good. You can never go wrong with foe.
I couldn't have done it without them and I'm so grateful for their hard work and generosity.
I really can't thank you enough for that.

title: have to / must
examples: So I had to make a quick pit stop at all of young after dinner.

title: had better / it's time
examples: It's time for the awards ceremony.

title: If I do / If I did
examples: If you have some kind of event, I really recommend working with red hyphen. They're awesome.

title: have something done
examples: Almost every pocha was packed and had a line but luckily we found an open seat at a Vietnamese restaurant.
